plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Sell SMH to reduce semiconductor concentration.
Trim QQQI and SPY to lower exposure and concentration.
Add QQQ to increase broad index exposure.
Buy XLV for defensive sector exposure.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation by reducing leverage and concentration.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for low monthly growth around 1.5%. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leveraged ETFs and sector concentration, holding more cash and broad index exposure for stability.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and sell SPAL to reduce high risk.
Trim SMH and QQQI to lower sector concentration and income exposure.
Trim SPY to address concentration.
Add to CRWD, a strong performer, to increase tech exposure.
Buy RTX for diversification and quality.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth by trimming leverage and concentration while adding to strong performers.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% monthly growth with a risk-first approach. It reduces leverage and concentration, blending broad index, quality tech, and a small allocation to a leveraged ETF.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) for aggressive growth.
Increase SMH to concentrate in semiconductors.
Trim SPY to reduce broad index exposure and free up capital.
Sell QQQI to exit income and focus on high-beta growth.
Add to CRWD, a strong performer, and buy PANW for high-scoring tech exposure.
This aggressive plan aims for higher growth by increasing leverage and concentration in high-scoring tech and semiconductor ETFs, with a smaller cash buffer.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly growth, accepting significantly higher potential drawdowns. It leans into leveraged ETFs and concentrated tech/semiconductor exposure, utilizing wider stops but demanding strict discipline due to amplified risk and ETF decay.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta single names (CRWD, SMH) to eliminate volatility.
Sell SMH to exit volatile sectors.
Significantly increase QQQI allocation to maximize income generation.
Increase SPY and QQQ allocations to provide a stable broad index base for income.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdowns over growth.
This income-focused plan aims for reliable monthly distributions around 1% by shifting to income-generating ETFs and broad index funds, minimizing volatility and capital risk.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% monthly distributions with a focus on capital preservation. It eliminates volatile assets, concentrating on income-focused ETFs and broad index funds for steady cash flow and low drawdowns.
